questions.question: Which query shows each student's name and score, replacing NULL scores with 0?

options.A: SELECT name, COALESCE(0, score) AS score FROM students;
options.B: SELECT name, COALESCE(score, 0) AS score FROM students;
options.C: SELECT name, REPLACE(score, NULL, 0) AS score FROM students;
options.D: SELECT name, NULLIF(score, 0) AS score FROM students;

questions.question: Which query shows each product's name and category_id, replacing NULL category_id with 99?

options.B: SELECT name, COALESCE(NULL, category_id) AS category_id FROM products;
options.C: SELECT name, COALESCE(category_id, 99) AS category_id FROM products;
options.D: SELECT name, CASE WHEN category_id = NULL THEN 99 ELSE category_id END FROM products;

questions.question: Which query labels each student as 'Pass' if their score is >= 60, otherwise 'Fail'?

options.A: SELECT name, CASE WHEN score => 60 THEN 'Pass' ELSE 'Fail' END AS result FROM students;
options.B: SELECT name, CASE score >= 60 THEN 'Pass' ELSE 'Fail' END AS result FROM students;
options.C: SELECT name, CASE WHEN score >= 60 THEN 'Pass' ELSE 'Fail' END AS result FROM students;
options.D: SELECT name, WHEN score >= 60 THEN 'Pass' ELSE 'Fail' END AS result FROM students;

questions.question: Which query assigns grades: 'A' (>=90), 'B' (>=75), 'C' (>=60), 'F' (below 60) to each student?

options.A: SELECT name, CASE WHEN score >= 90 THEN 'A' WHEN score >= 75 THEN 'B' WHEN score >= 60 THEN 'C' ELSE 'F' END AS grade FROM students;
options.B: SELECT name, CASE WHEN score >= 60 THEN 'C' WHEN score >= 75 THEN 'B' WHEN score >= 90 THEN 'A' ELSE 'F' END AS grade FROM students;
options.C: SELECT name, CASE WHEN score = 90 THEN 'A' WHEN score = 75 THEN 'B' WHEN score = 60 THEN 'C' ELSE 'F' END AS grade FROM students;
options.D: SELECT name, CASE score WHEN 90 THEN 'A' WHEN 75 THEN 'B' WHEN 60 THEN 'C' ELSE 'F' END AS grade FROM students;

questions.question: Which query returns product names and their category name, but only for products whose price is greater than 100?

options.A: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p INNER JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id HAVING p.price > 100;
options.B: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p INNER JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.price > 100;
options.C: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p INNER JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE p.price > 100;
options.D: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE p.price > 100;

questions.question: Which query shows all products and their sale date, but only where the sale date is '2024-01-11'? (Products with no sale on that date should still appear.)

options.A: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id WHERE s.sale_date = '2024-01-11';
options.B: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p LEFT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id AND s.sale_date = '2024-01-11';
options.C: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p LEFT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id WHERE s.sale_date = '2024-01-11';
options.D: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM sales s LEFT JOIN products p ON p.product_id = s.product_id WHERE s.sale_date = '2024-01-11';

questions.question: Which query returns the total quantity sold for each product_id?

options.A: SELECT product_id, COUNT(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales GROUP BY product_id;
options.B: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales;
options.C: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales GROUP BY qty;
options.D: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales GROUP BY product_id;

questions.question: Which query returns the highest price for each category_id?

options.A: SELECT category_id, MAX(price) AS max_price FROM products GROUP BY category_id;
options.B: SELECT category_id, MAX(price) AS max_price FROM products;
options.C: SELECT category_id, MAX(price) AS max_price FROM products GROUP BY price;
options.D: SELECT MAX(price) AS max_price FROM products GROUP BY category_id;

questions.question: Which query counts how many products belong to each category_id, including the row where category_id is NULL?

options.A: SELECT category_id, COUNT(category_id) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category_id;
options.B: SELECT category_id, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products;
options.C: SELECT COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category_id;
options.D: SELECT category_id, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category_id;

questions.question: Which query finds product_ids whose total quantity sold is greater than 5?

options.A: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) FROM sales WHERE SUM(qty) > 5 GROUP BY product_id;
options.B: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales GROUP BY product_id HAVING qty > 5;
options.C: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales GROUP BY product_id HAVING SUM(qty) > 5;
options.D: SELECT product_id, SUM(qty) AS total_qty FROM sales HAVING SUM(qty) > 5;

questions.question: Which query shows each sale's product name and quantity sold? (Only show rows where there is a matching product.)

options.A: SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM products p LEFT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id;
options.B: SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM sales s INNER JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.product_id;
options.C: SELECT p.name, s.qty FROM products p, sales s;
options.D: SELECT name, qty FROM products INNER JOIN sales;

questions.question: Which query shows all products and their sale date, including products that have no sales (showing NULL for sale_date)?

options.A: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p INNER JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id;
options.B: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM sales s LEFT JOIN products p ON s.product_id = p.product_id;
options.C: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p RIGHT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id;
options.D: SELECT p.name, s.sale_date FROM products p LEFT JOIN sales s ON p.product_id = s.product_id;

questions.question: Which query shows each product name together with its category name? (Only show products that have a matching category.)

options.A: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p LEFT JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id;
options.B: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p INNER JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id;
options.C: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p INNER JOIN categories c ON p.name = c.category_name;
options.D: SELECT p.name, c.category_name FROM products p, categories c;

questions.question: Which query lists category_ids that have more than 1 product?

options.A: SELECT category_id FROM products WHERE COUNT(*) > 1 GROUP BY category_id;
options.B: SELECT category_id, COUNT(*) FROM products GROUP BY category_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
options.C: SELECT category_id, COUNT(*) FROM products HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
options.D: SELECT category_id FROM products GROUP BY category_id HAVING category_id > 1;

questions.question: The sales table sometimes has NULL in the qty column. Which query shows all sale records, replacing NULL qty with 0?

options.A: SELECT sale_id, product_id, COALESCE(0, qty) AS qty FROM sales;
options.B: SELECT sale_id, product_id, NULLIF(qty, 0) AS qty FROM sales;
options.C: SELECT sale_id, product_id, COALESCE(qty, 0) AS qty FROM sales;
options.D: SELECT sale_id, product_id, REPLACE(qty, NULL, 0) AS qty FROM sales;
